title: Adding sortable column: backend index + API param + UI control + URL sync
scenario_context: Table needs sort by date/name/status. Touches all layers including DB index.
verification_chain: Layer 8: column header clickable with sort indicator
what_breaks_naive_approach: Add sort by createdAt; table query slow.
reasoning: Whitelist allowed fields. Index per. Encode in URL: ?sort=createdAt:desc. Header shows arrow.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Whitelist via Zod enum. 2) Index per column. 3) URL state for sharing.

title: Server returns 200 with success:false body
scenario_context: Legacy API returns 200 with {success: false, error: ...}. Newer code expects exceptions on failure.
verification_chain: Layer 6: fetcher inspects body, throws if success:false
what_breaks_naive_approach: Code does if(res.ok) treat as success — silently uses error data.
reasoning: Wrapper recognizes legacy shape, throws. Migrate endpoints to proper HTTP semantics.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Adapter pattern. 2) Migrate not just adapt. 3) Telemetry-driven sunset.

title: Type guards generated from Zod schemas
scenario_context: Need 'isUser(x): x is User' type guards. Hand-written can drift from Zod.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Guard says yes; runtime crashes.
reasoning: Generate guard from schema: returns true/false based on safeParse.
frontend_solution: // uses guards for narrowing
test_strategy: Random objects; guard agrees with schema
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Generate not hand-write. 2) Single source. 3) Performance considered.

title: Tenant resource quota enforcement with eviction
scenario_context: Free plan = 100MB storage. Hard limit. New uploads fail. Need: clear UX + cleanup options.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User uploads 50 small files, ok. Tries 1 big file — fails mid-upload.
reasoning: Pre-flight check against quota. Break down: by file type, oldest, largest. Bulk delete options.
frontend_solution: // client/components/StorageDashboard.tsx — bar of usage by type, list of largest/oldest
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Pre-flight prevents partial upload. 2) Breakdown UX. 3) Cleanup tools.

title: Promo code application with validation and one-time use
scenario_context: Marketing campaigns issue codes. Need: validate, apply discount, prevent reuse, track redemptions.
verification_chain: Layer 1: promo_codes table with maxRedemptions, expiresAt
Layer 8: code input with live check
what_breaks_naive_approach: User shares code on Twitter; thousands redeem.
reasoning: Code is random + unique. Each redemption logged. maxRedemptions enforced via atomic update.
frontend_solution: // client/components/PromoInput.tsx — live validation, success shows discount
test_strategy: Concurrent redeem of limited code; only N succeed
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Atomic redemption. 2) Per-tenant limit. 3) Stripe coupon integration.

title: Email verification with resend cooldown
scenario_context: Email verify link sent. User asks for resend. Naive: spam. Need cooldown + max attempts.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User clicks resend 10x; emails flood.
reasoning: Cooldown stored. Resend disabled until expired. Counter for abuse detection.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Cooldown + daily limit. 2) UX countdown. 3) Token expiry.

title: SCIM provisioning for enterprise SSO
scenario_context: Enterprise customers use Okta/Azure AD. SCIM auto-provisions users when added to groups.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User leaves company; remains in app forever.
reasoning: Implement SCIM 2.0 spec subset (Users, Groups). IdP-provisioned token per tenant. Deactivate = soft (login blocked).
frontend_solution: // admin UI for setup
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Standard protocol. 2) Soft deactivate. 3) IdP-managed lifecycle.

title: Recharts gradient fill for area chart
scenario_context: Visual polish: gradient from primary color to transparent.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Plain solid fill looks dated.
reasoning: <defs><linearGradient id='myGradient' x1='0' x2='0' y1='0' y2='1'><stop offset='0%' stopColor='var(--primary)'/><stop offset='100%' stopColor='transparent'/></linearGradient></defs>. fill='url(#myGradient)'.
frontend_solution: // snippet inside ResponsiveContainer><AreaChart>\n<defs><linearGradient id={`gradient-${id}`} x1="0" x2="0" y1="0" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stopColor="var(--chart-line)" stopOpacity={0.8}/><stop offset="100%" stopColor="var(--chart-line)" stopOpacity={0}/></linearGradient></defs>\n<Area dataKey="value" fill={`url(#gradient-${id})`} stroke="var(--chart-line)" />
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Unique id per instance. 2) Theme CSS vars. 3) Subtle but polished.

title: Visx sankey for flow visualization
scenario_context: User journey: from acquisition source through conversion stages.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Pie chart of stages loses flow.
reasoning: d3-sankey computes positions. Render rects + paths. Hover dims unrelated.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) d3-sankey + visx. 2) Hover focus. 3) Bounded extent.

title: Chart.js with custom canvas overlay for game-like animations
scenario_context: Beyond charts — animated trading floor visualization. Chart.js underlying with custom overlay layer.
what_breaks_naive_approach: jQuery animate alongside chart — desync.
reasoning: Plugin draws on same canvas; rAF for state-driven animation.
frontend_solution: // see e2e-104 — extend with rAF for animated annotations
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Same canvas. 2) rAF. 3) Pause on hidden.

title: ECharts multi-axis time chart with synchronized cursors
scenario_context: Two stacked time charts; cursor on one shows on the other.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Cursor on one chart leaves other dim.
reasoning: echarts.connect(group) syncs tooltips across charts with same group.
frontend_solution: // useEffect(() => { echarts.connect('dashboard-group'); }, []);
test_strategy: Hover one, assert tooltip on other
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Native connect API. 2) Group scope. 3) Disconnect on unmount.

title: Tremor dashboard with date range picker shared
scenario_context: Dashboard cards all filter by date range. Date picker at top.
verification_chain: Layer 9: all cards refetch on change
what_breaks_naive_approach: User changes range; only some cards update.
reasoning: Shared context. Presets (7d, 30d, 90d, custom). URL syncs. React Query invalidates per range.
frontend_solution: // client/components/DashboardHeader.tsx with DateRangePicker, dispatches to filter context
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Shared context. 2) Presets. 3) URL sync.

title: Reduced motion: respect prefers-reduced-motion
scenario_context: Some users disable animations OS-wide. Charts should respect.
verification_chain: Layer 8: chart isAnimationActive driven by query
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Disabled on first load only — not reactive
what_breaks_naive_approach: Users with vestibular sensitivity nauseated.
reasoning: useMediaQuery('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') → isAnimationActive=false.
frontend_solution: // usage\nconst reducedMotion = useMediaQuery('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)');\n<Line isAnimationActive={!reducedMotion} />
test_strategy: Mock media query; assert no animation
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) OS setting respected. 2) Reactive. 3) Across all UI.

title: Adding tags: schema, indexing, query, UI input, filter
scenario_context: Tags on projects. Touch all layers: schema, indexes for tag queries, multi-select UI, filter chips.
what_breaks_naive_approach: 100k projects with tag query without index — 2s.
reasoning: Array index on tags. Lowercase normalization. Autocomplete from existing tags.
backend_solution: // ProjectModel.schema.index({ tenantId: 1, tags: 1 });\n// query: ProjectModel.find({ tenantId, tags: { $in: queryTags } })\n// suggest endpoint
frontend_solution: // TagPicker component with autocomplete + chip display
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Indexed. 2) Normalized. 3) Autocomplete.

title: What if scheduled job is missed (cron skipped)
scenario_context: Daily summary email job. Server down at scheduled time. Naive: missed forever.
verification_chain: Layer 3: catch-up logic on next run
what_breaks_naive_approach: Customer expects daily summary; receives nothing for 3 days.
reasoning: Each run records timestamp. Next run checks for missed (lastRun + interval < now). Catch up with batch or single 'missed days' email.
frontend_solution: // N/A
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) State table. 2) Catch-up logic. 3) Honest user message.

title: Generic CRUD service factory with typed methods
scenario_context: Many similar CRUD services. DRY via factory while preserving types.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Forcing all entities into one shape
what_breaks_naive_approach: Each CRUD reimplemented.
reasoning: Generic factory; entity-specific extends.
frontend_solution: // matching client
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Generic factory. 2) Composition over inheritance. 3) Extensible.

title: Tenant data export via background job
scenario_context: GDPR — user requests data export. Export is large; cannot inline.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User clicks export; 5 minutes later — request timeout.
reasoning: Job creates ZIP in S3, generates pre-signed URL expiring in 24h, emails user.
frontend_solution: // poll status; show progress + download link
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Async via queue. 2) Pre-signed URL. 3) Progress + email notification.

title: Annual vs monthly toggle with savings indicator
scenario_context: Plan page: monthly $X, annual $Y. Savings unclear.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Users don't switch to annual; revenue lost.
reasoning: Show both with explicit savings (e.g., 'Save $X / 20%'). Confirm long-term commitment.
frontend_solution: // PlanCard with toggle and savings
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Explicit savings. 2) Both visible. 3) Commitment clear.

title: Progressive disclosure based on plan tier
scenario_context: Pro features hidden from Free users — but discoverable so they know what to upgrade for.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User doesn't know pro features exist; no upsell.
reasoning: Show feature with lock icon + 'Pro' badge. Click → preview/demo + upgrade CTA.
test_strategy: Free user sees locked; Pro doesn't
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Visible discoverable. 2) Demo bridge. 3) Conversion-tracked.

title: Service account vs user account distinction
scenario_context: API access via dedicated service account, not personal user. Different lifecycle, audit, perms.
verification_chain: Layer 3: service accounts can't log in via web UI
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Personal user for integrations — leaves on quit
what_breaks_naive_approach: Employee leaves; their API integrations break.
reasoning: Service accounts are entities owned by tenant, not user. Survive personnel changes. Restricted to API access.
frontend_solution: // admin UI for service accounts
test_strategy: Service account API works; web login blocked
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Lifecycle independent of personnel. 2) Restricted to API. 3) Audit-distinct.

title: Recharts mini-sparklines in table rows
scenario_context: Data table with trend column. Each row's last 7 days as a tiny sparkline.
what_breaks_naive_approach: 100 rows × full chart = 100 ResponsiveContainers — slow.
reasoning: Tiny LineChart per row with width/height fixed (no responsive). Memoize.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Fixed size avoids ResponsiveContainer overhead. 2) Memoized. 3) isAnimationActive=false.

title: Visx pattern fills as redundant encoding
scenario_context: Color-blind: rely on patterns + color. Visx pattern fills.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Color-blind exec can't distinguish.
reasoning: @visx/pattern provides patterns. Apply via fill='url(#pattern-id)'. Legend rectangles use same patterns.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Redundant encoding. 2) Legend mirrors patterns. 3) Pattern + color.

title: Chart.js dataset visibility toggle via legend with persistence
scenario_context: User hides 'noise' series. Returns next day — series visible again. Persist preference.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User re-hides every session.
reasoning: Override legend onClick: update dataset.hidden + persist. On mount, apply persisted.
frontend_solution: // hidden state from localStorage on init; onClick updates + persists
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Preference persisted. 2) Key per chart. 3) Reset option.

title: ECharts custom toolbox: download + reset zoom + magicType
scenario_context: Power users want chart export to PNG, switch line/bar, reset zoom — built-in toolbox.
what_breaks_naive_approach: User asks for PNG; manual screenshot.
reasoning: toolbox config with saveAsImage, dataZoom, restore, magicType.
frontend_solution: // option.toolbox = { feature: { saveAsImage: {}, dataZoom: {}, restore: {}, magicType: { type: ['line','bar'] } } };
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Native toolbox > custom. 2) Mobile-aware. 3) Per-action telemetry.

title: Tremor SearchSelect with virtualization for large lists
scenario_context: Selecting from 10k options. Without virtualization, browser stalls.
what_breaks_naive_approach: 10k DOM nodes freezes.
reasoning: Server search returns top 50. Virtuoso renders only visible. Keyboard arrow nav.
frontend_solution: // extend e2e-119 with Virtuoso for list rendering
edge_cases_addressed: Selected item not in current page — preserve
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Server search. 2) Virtualization. 3) Keyboard accessible.

title: Loading state for chart matching final dimensions
scenario_context: Chart loads asynchronously. Skeleton must match aspect ratio + bar count.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Page content jumps when chart loads.
reasoning: Skeleton with same height/width as chart, with placeholder bars/lines.
frontend_solution: // see e2e-009 ChartSkeleton
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Dimensions match. 2) Pulse respects motion. 3) CLS=0.

title: Adding webhook signature verification with rotation
scenario_context: Webhook secret leak — need rotation without downtime. Support multiple secrets temporarily.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Rotate secret — all webhooks fail for hours.
reasoning: Multiple active secrets. New secret accepted alongside old. Cut over when traffic confirms new key used.
frontend_solution: // admin: regenerate secret with overlap
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Multiple active secrets. 2) timingSafeEqual. 3) Telemetry-driven cutover.

title: What if external service is rate limiting us — circuit + queue
scenario_context: Third-party (e.g., email provider) rate limits us. Combine circuit breaker + queue.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Emails lost.
reasoning: Persistent queue. Worker respects rate. Circuit when sustained.
frontend_solution: // N/A
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Queue + circuit. 2) Persistent. 3) Priority lanes.

title: Auto-generated React Query hooks from schema
scenario_context: For each query, write a useQuery wrapper. Repetitive. Generate from schema.
what_breaks_naive_approach: 50 hooks duplicating boilerplate.
reasoning: createQuery<Input, Output>({ path, schema }) returns useQuery hook with key + fn.
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Factory DRY. 2) Type-safe. 3) Schema validates.

title: Tenant-scoped feature usage tracking
scenario_context: Show admin which features their team uses. Track per feature per tenant.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Admin can't see what's used.
reasoning: Lightweight middleware logs feature usage event. Aggregated daily.
frontend_solution: // admin dashboard
what_distinguishes_senior_thinking: 1) Tenant-scoped. 2) Sampled. 3) Anonymized.
